questions.question: Prepare the cash book for Kalima for September 2024.
questions.answer: Cash book: Debit side - Sept 1 Balance b/d $240, Sept 3 Sales $478, Sept 14 Elizah $13, Sept 16 Cash $120 = $851. Credit side - Sept 1 Balance b/d $890, Sept 2 Stationery $82, Sept 3 Badr $30, Sept 5 Wages $1390, Sept 10 Drawings $150, Sept 16 Bank $120, Sept 21 Gulnar $4196, Sept 30 Balance c/d $2029 = $7788. Oct 1 Balance b/d $516, Oct 1 Balance b/d $2029.

questions.question: State the balance on the bank column at 30 September 2024.
questions.answer: Bank overdraft.

questions.question: Prepare the Elizah account for Kalima for September 2024.
questions.answer: Elizah account: Debit side - Sept 1 Balance b/d $520, Sept 23 Sales $1450 = $1970. Credit side - Sept 14 Bank $507, Discount allowed $13, Sept 29 Sales returns $325, Sept 30 Balance c/d $1125 = $1970. Oct 1 Balance b/d $1125.

questions.question: State one current asset from the cash book.
questions.answer: Cash.

questions.question: Calculate the capital at 1 April 2023 and at 31 March 2024 for Ben.
Prepare the capital account for Ben for the year ended 31 March 2024.
questions.answer: Capital account: Debit side - Drawings $620 = $620. Credit side - Capital at 1 April 2023 $259900, Capital introduced $10000, Profit for the year $20620 = $290520. Balance c/d $289900. Capital at 31 March 2024 $289900.

questions.question: Calculate the purchases for Ben for the year ended 31 March 2024.
questions.answer: Payments to credit suppliers $224700, Add closing trade payables $19700 = $244400, Less opening trade payables $24750 = $219650, Less drawings $620 = Purchases $219030.

questions.question: Calculate the total sales for Ben for the year ended 31 March 2024.
questions.answer: Payments received from credit customers $452000, Add closing trade receivables $43400 = $495400, Less opening trade receivables $39600 = Total credit sales $455800, Cash sales $21000 = Total sales for the year $476800.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of employing a book-keeper and make a recommendation.
questions.answer: Advantages: Preparation of financial statements would be quicker/easier, Better control over business activities/able to track transactions/income and expenses, Chances of fraud reduced/errors may be reduced, Comparison with previous years/aids decision making, Information required by a bank/investor readily available, Allow Ben time to concentrate on other business activities. Disadvantages: Salary of book-keeper to pay, Profit may not be adequate to cover the cost of employing the book-keeper, Additional costs on top of salary paid, Problems of recruiting suitable/trained candidate. Recommendation: Should employ a book-keeper.

questions.question: State the accounting principle that requires inventory to be valued at the lower of cost and net realisable value.
questions.answer: Prudence or Matching/accruals.

questions.question: Show the effect of correcting the inventory valuation error on profit for the year, trade receivables, and cash at bank.
questions.answer: Profit for the year - decrease, Trade receivables - decrease, Cash at bank - no effect.

questions.question: Prepare the cafe income statement for Sports T club for the year ended 31 December 2023.
questions.answer: Cafe sales $27000, Cost of sales: Opening inventory $1290, Purchases of food & drink $8220 = $9510, Less Closing inventory $1340 = $8170, Gross profit $18830. Expenses: Wages - cafe assistant $14352 = $14352, Profit for the year - cafe $4478.

questions.question: Prepare the subscriptions account for Sports T club for the year ended 31 December 2023.
questions.answer: Subscriptions account: Debit side - Jan 1 2023 Balance b/d $1520, Dec 31 2023 Balance c/d $2800 = $4320. Credit side - Dec 31 2023 Bank or Cash $162000, Dec 31 2023 Income & Expenditure Account $158960 = $320960. Balance c/d $1280. Jan 1 2024 Balance b/d $1280, Jan 1 2024 Balance b/d $2800.

questions.question: Prepare the income and expenditure account for Sports T club for the year ended 31 December 2023.
questions.answer: Income: Profit from the cafe $4478, Subscriptions $158960, Bank interest $720 = $164158. Expenditure: Wages: Sports coaches $58220, Administration salaries $31720 = $89940, Rent (16250 - 1250) $15000, Sports equipment hire $34800, Rates & insurance $3200, Accountancy fees $2400 = $145340, Surplus for the year $18818.

questions.question: State one difference between a public limited company and a private limited company.
questions.answer: A public limited company is allowed to offer its shares to the public whereas a private limited company cannot, or A public limited company has to publish its financial statements whereas a private limited company does not.

questions.question: Explain the difference between issued share capital and called up share capital.
questions.answer: Issued share capital is the amount of share capital issued to the shareholders, Called up share capital is the part of issued share capital for which payment has been requested from the shareholders.

questions.question: State two advantages of a limited company compared to a partnership.
questions.answer: Liability for the debts of the company is limited to the amount invested by the shareholder, Separate legal identity/legal action cannot be taken against the individual shareholders of the company, Access to additional capital/easier to obtain loans, Has continuity of existence.

questions.question: Prepare the statement of changes in equity for Y Limited for the year ended 31 December 2023.
questions.answer: Statement of changes in equity: On 1 January 2023 - Ordinary Share Capital $800000, General Reserve $78000, Retained Earnings $62000, Total $940000. Profit for the year $118500, Dividend paid (final) $43000, Dividend paid (interim) $40000, Transfer to general reserve $32000. On 31 December 2023 - Ordinary Share Capital $800000, General Reserve $110000, Retained Earnings $65500, Total $975500.

questions.question: Prepare the equity section of the statement of financial position for Y Limited at 31 December 2023.
questions.answer: Equity: Ordinary Shares $800000, General reserve $110000, Retained earnings $65500 = $975500.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of issuing debentures or ordinary shares and make a recommendation.
questions.answer: Issuing debentures: Annual interest is payable, Are a liability/are a loan/must be repaid, If company is wound up they are repaid before ordinary shareholders, Debenture holders are not members of the company/cannot vote, Issue of debentures will not dilute the control of the existing ordinary shareholders, Interest is a fixed amount (and so can be budgeted for), Interest must be paid irrespective of profits, May be secured on the non-current assets of the company, Issue may not raise adequate funds. Issuing ordinary shares: A dividend may be paid, Do not have to be repaid, If company is wound up they are repaid after debenture holders, Shares will carry same voting rights/rank equally as existing shares, May dilute control of existing shareholders, Dividend is not fixed (and may depend on profit levels), Directors can decide on amount of dividend they will pay, May take longer to raise the funds, Issue may not raise adequate funds. Recommendation: Should issue ordinary shares.

questions.question: State where debentures are shown in the statement of financial position.
questions.answer: Non-current liabilities/Long-term liabilities.

questions.question: Calculate the inventory turnover, trade receivables turnover, and trade payables turnover for the year ended 31 December 2023.
questions.answer: Inventory turnover = (24074 + 278429 - 25600) / ((24074 + 25600) / 2) = 276903 / 24837 = 11.15 times. Trade receivables turnover = (42375 / 392600) x 365 = 40 days. Trade payables turnover = (21603 / 278429) x 365 = 29 days.

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of using a debt collection company and make a recommendation.
questions.answer: Advantages: Reduced administration - debt collection company will manage debt collection, Owner can spend more time on other areas of the business, Money received from credit customers quicker/improved cashflow/improved liquidity/trade receivables turnover will improve, Reduced risk of irrecoverable debts, Reduced need to borrow to finance working capital. Disadvantages: Cost/fees of debt collection company, May damage relationship with customer, Credit sales may reduce, Maybe a lack of communication between the agency and the owner/may not be effective in improving debt collection. Recommendation: Should use a debt collection company.

questions.question: Suggest two methods Azim could use to improve the collection of amounts owed by credit customers.
questions.answer: Improve credit control policy, Issue regular statements & invoices, Offer cash discount for prompt payment, Charge interest on overdue accounts.

questions.question: Suggest one way Azim could improve his gross profit margin.
questions.answer: Reduce cost of purchases, Increase selling price, Changing the proportions of types of goods sold.

questions.question: Suggest one way Azim could improve his profit margin.
questions.answer: Improve gross profit, Control/reduce overall expenses, Increasing other income.

questions.question: Prepare the journal entry to correct the error in Azim's books.
questions.answer: Journal: Debit Drawings $340, Credit Purchases $340.

questions.question: Show the effect of correcting this error on gross profit and closing capital.
questions.answer: Gross profit - increase, Closing capital - decrease.
